title: taraxacum subnaevosum
intro: Taraxacum subnaevosum is a lesser-known species of dandelion, a flowering plant in the sunflower family, known for its yellow flowers and jagged leaves. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum subnaevosum are attributed to its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain vitamins A, C, and K, as well as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Taraxacum subnaevosum include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce blood pressure, and support liver function, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Taraxacum subnaevosum can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or teas made from its roots, leaves, or flowers, and can be used to support overall well-being and health.
